tweets.title: R to @OsintTV: More details https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/air-force-iaf-document-36-rafales-pakistan-operation-sindoor-loss-claims-2932499-2026-06-23
tweets.content:
More details

indiatoday.in/india/story/ai…

Link

Pak's Rafale loss claims busted again, Air Force document shows all 36 in service

An Indian Air Force tender seeking bridge support for all 36 Rafale fighter jets contradicts Pakistan's repeated claims that India lost multiple Rafales during Operation Sindoor.

indiatoday.in
tweets.author: @OsintTV

tweets.title: 🚨 Official IAF Records Sink Pakistan's Rafale Claims 📍 Pakistan's claims of multiple Rafale losses during Operation Sindoor have been contradicted by official Indian Air Force records. 📍 An Air Headquarters Request for Proposal (RFP), accessed through a "Reply to Request for Information under the RTI Act, 2005", confirms that all 36 Rafale fighter jets inducted by the IAF remain operational. 📍 The fleet strength was recorded at 36 aircraft as of June 18, 2026. 📍 The disclosure effectively dismantles months of claims and propaganda surrounding alleged Rafale losses. 📍 India inducted all 36 Rafales between 2020 and 2022. 📍 The aircraft are currently operated by No. 17 Squadron "Golden Arrows" at Ambala and No. 101 Squadron "Falcons" at Hasimara.
tweets.content:
🚨 Official IAF Records Sink Pakistan's Rafale Claims

📍 Pakistan's claims of multiple Rafale losses during Operation Sindoor have been contradicted by official Indian Air Force records.

📍 An Air Headquarters Request for Proposal (RFP), accessed through a "Reply to Request for Information under the RTI Act, 2005", confirms that all 36 Rafale fighter jets inducted by the IAF remain operational.

📍 The fleet strength was recorded at 36 aircraft as of June 18, 2026.

📍 The disclosure effectively dismantles months of claims and propaganda surrounding alleged Rafale losses.

📍 India inducted all 36 Rafales between 2020 and 2022.

📍 The aircraft are currently operated by No. 17 Squadron "Golden Arrows" at Ambala and No. 101 Squadron "Falcons" at Hasimara.
tweets.author: @OsintTV

tweets.title: Singapore High Commissioner Wong meets CDS General NS Raja Subramani
tweets.content:
Singapore High Commissioner Wong meets CDS General NS Raja Subramani

Singapore in India (@SGinIndia)

Had an introductory call today on Chief of Defence Staff of the Indian Armed Forces, General NS Raja Subramani. Congratulated him on his recent appointment and reflected on the breadth of long-running defence cooperation between the armed forces of both countries through major exercises, exchanges, and established dialogues. Also shared views on furthering this strong partnership under General Subramani’s leadership. - HC Wong
@SpokespersonMoD @HQ_IDS_India @DefenceMinIndia

tweets.title: Q: India will sell Brahmos missiles to the UAE according to some reports. Can you confirm? "We have close relations with the UAE in the defense sector, and we want to strengthen this relationship further. As for any specific defense platform, only the Ministry of Defence can share those details." MEA.
tweets.content:
Q: India will sell Brahmos missiles to the UAE according to some reports. Can you confirm?

"We have close relations with the UAE in the defense sector, and we want to strengthen this relationship further. As for any specific defense platform, only the Ministry of Defence can share those details." MEA.


Video
Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

India and the UAE in early-stage talks to export the BrahMos after its steller performance in Operation Sindoor.

India is in early-stage talks with the UAE to export the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile. The countries are also discussing the sale of Akashteer, an automated air defense system developed by India which was extensively deployed against Pakistan during Operation Sindoor. This follows a massive jump in India's defense exports, which crossed $4 billion in early 2026.


Video

— https://nitter.net/NetramDefence/status/2069299357246267598#m
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: R to @sidhant: Quote "We have seen some reports on the matter. Such remarks are desperate attempts by Pakistan to cover-up its own failings and deflect attention away from its human rights abuses. We categorically reject these fabricated claims with the contempt they deserve. The ongoing protests in PoK are a direct consequence of Pakistan’s decades-long policy of systemic economic exploitation, denial of fundamental rights, and administrative oppression in areas under its illegal and forcible occupation. The Pakistani state has responded with extreme police brutality, blocking of essential supplies and medicines, internet blackouts, and use of lethal force against unarmed civilians. We hope the international community will hold Pakistan accountable for its misdeeds and abuses."
tweets.content:
Quote

"We have seen some reports on the matter. Such remarks are desperate attempts by Pakistan to cover-up its own failings and deflect attention away from its human rights abuses. We categorically reject these fabricated claims with the contempt they deserve. The ongoing protests in PoK are a direct consequence of Pakistan’s decades-long policy of systemic economic exploitation, denial of fundamental rights, and administrative oppression in areas under its illegal and forcible occupation. The Pakistani state has responded with extreme police brutality, blocking of essential supplies and medicines, internet blackouts, and use of lethal force against unarmed civilians. We hope the international community will hold Pakistan accountable for its misdeeds and abuses."

tweets.title: BREAKING: CISF to Link Facial Recognition Network with Intelligence Grid The Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) plans to connect facial recognition cameras at major airports and strategic installations to a new data fusion centre in Delhi, significantly enhancing surveillance and real-time threat detection. Facial recognition systems at Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Chennai, and Kolkata airports to be integrated with NATGRID.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: CISF to Link Facial Recognition Network with Intelligence Grid

The Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) plans to connect facial recognition cameras at major airports and strategic installations to a new data fusion centre in Delhi, significantly enhancing surveillance and real-time threat detection.

Facial recognition systems at Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Chennai, and Kolkata airports to be integrated with NATGRID.

tweets.title: The U.S. has temporarily suspended sanctions on Iranian oil exports for 60 days after Tehran reportedly agreed to allow IAEA inspectors back into the country and maintain free transit through the Strait of Hormuz. The move is being framed by Washington as a confidence-building measure toward a broader nuclear and regional security agreement. Oil markets are closely watching the development.
tweets.content:
The U.S. has temporarily suspended sanctions on Iranian oil exports for 60 days after Tehran reportedly agreed to allow IAEA inspectors back into the country and maintain free transit through the Strait of Hormuz.

The move is being framed by Washington as a confidence-building measure toward a broader nuclear and regional security agreement. Oil markets are closely watching the development.

tweets.title: The only reason I look the other way when DefX talks about schemes that provide financial assistance to the country’s marginalised communities is because that is a very small cost to pay to keep the right people in the right offices. Always notice one thing: Pakistanis, Bangladeshis, and other adversaries of the Indian state rarely attack the Indian Armed Forces or the intelligence community directly. They attack the political structure because they know where the backing that enables these institutions to do what they believe is best for the country comes from. We could become extremely vulnerable within a couple of years if we do not have strong and stable leadership at the Centre for at least the next decade. There are too many wolves around us, lads.

tweets.title: BREAKING: Jaipur Woman Arrested Over Jaish-e-Mohammed Links The Rajasthan ATS has arrested a woman from Jaipur for maintaining contact with members of the Pakistan-based terror outfit Jaish-e-Mohammed and other anti-national elements. She has been booked under the UAPA, with investigators examining her digital communications, social media activity, and foreign contacts. According to reports, the suspect was preparing for travel to Pakistan and had undergone religious conversion. Authorities are also probing online accounts, deleted data, and possible links to overseas operatives.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: Jaipur Woman Arrested Over Jaish-e-Mohammed Links

The Rajasthan ATS has arrested a woman from Jaipur for maintaining contact with members of the Pakistan-based terror outfit Jaish-e-Mohammed and other anti-national elements.

She has been booked under the UAPA, with investigators examining her digital communications, social media activity, and foreign contacts.

According to reports, the suspect was preparing for travel to Pakistan and had undergone religious conversion. Authorities are also probing online accounts, deleted data, and possible links to overseas operatives.

tweets.title: ‘Won't return to pre-war conditions’: Iran says Hormuz will be administered by them Iran's chief negotiator and parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf on Tuesday said the Strait of Hormuz would remain under Tehran's administration and would not return to its pre-war status. Ghalibaf stated that "the Strait of Hormuz will never return to its pre-war conditions and will be administered by the Islamic Republic of Iran,” AFP reported.
tweets.content:
‘Won't return to pre-war conditions’: Iran says Hormuz will be administered by them

Iran's chief negotiator and parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf on Tuesday said the Strait of Hormuz would remain under Tehran's administration and would not return to its pre-war status.

Ghalibaf stated that "the Strait of Hormuz will never return to its pre-war conditions and will be administered by the Islamic Republic of Iran,” AFP reported.

tweets.content:

tweets.title: Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief: 22nd Jun 2026: - We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East - ⁠Follow us for daily Brief ● The IDF eliminated 3 armed terrorists from Hamas’ military wing who were attempting to carry out attacks against IDF troops. ● Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Baqaei stated that a threatening US statement during the four-party session prompted Iran to declare it would not continue the talks for now. ● The US Treasury Department issued a 60-day general license, effective until 21 August 2026, authorizing the production, delivery, and sale of Iranian-origin petroleum. ● Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesman announced that Tehran’s interaction with the IAEA will be subject to approval by Iran’s Parliament and the Supreme National Security Council. ● The French and Qatari-owned gas carriers WADI AL SAIL and MEKAINES were allowed to transit the Strait of Hormuz this morning, both en route to Ras Laffan LNG terminal. END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
22nd Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IDF eliminated 3 armed terrorists from Hamas’ military wing who were attempting to carry out attacks against IDF troops.

● Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Baqaei stated that a threatening US statement during the four-party session prompted Iran to declare it would not continue the talks for now.

● The US Treasury Department issued a 60-day general license, effective until 21 August 2026, authorizing the production, delivery, and sale of Iranian-origin petroleum.

● Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesman announced that Tehran’s interaction with the IAEA will be subject to approval by Iran’s Parliament and the Supreme National Security Council.

● The French and Qatari-owned gas carriers WADI AL SAIL and MEKAINES were allowed to transit the Strait of Hormuz this morning, both en route to Ras Laffan LNG terminal.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
21st Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● US VP Vance arrived at Bürgenstock, Switzerland, for direct talks with an Iranian delegation led by Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf and FM Abbas Araghchi.

● Following Iran’s announcement closing the Strait, no vessels have traversed the waterway, despite contradicting US claims.

● Three Chinese-linked oil tankers - ELVA, VIRGO, and VIGOR - laden with Iranian oil are heading toward the Strait of Hormuz.

● Pakistan's FM Ishaq Dar stated that an agreement has been reached between the US and Iran to lower the enrichment level of Tehran’s highly enriched uranium stockpile.

● A product tanker was approached by a skiff with five armed individuals attempting to board, 50 nautical miles southeast of Yemen.

END OF THE BRIEF

tweets.title: For more than a week, I’ve been sitting on this because the same people have spent years branding anyone who asks questions for a better IAF as “anti-Rafale” and, at times, even “anti-Air Force.” Yes, the Rafale engine PBL has expired. A bridge arrangement is being signed while a larger contract is negotiated and the entire PBL framework is renegotiated. But now that this document is all over the place, there’s no point pretending otherwise.
tweets.content:
For more than a week, I’ve been sitting on this because the same people have spent years branding anyone who asks questions for a better IAF as “anti-Rafale” and, at times, even “anti-Air Force.”

Yes, the Rafale engine PBL has expired. A bridge arrangement is being signed while a larger contract is negotiated and the entire PBL framework is renegotiated.

But now that this document is all over the place, there’s no point pretending otherwise.

tweets.title: A Navy veteran once explained the difference between an SSN and an SSBN to a group of fighter-jet enthusiasts: SSN = Fighter jet SSBN = Bomber An SSN is fast, agile, relatively smaller, and most importantly extremely quiet. An SSBN is a nightmare in hibernation. And once awakened, the target country may end up in hibernation instead. That’s why the journey from SSBN to SSN is a significant technological leap. Going the other way around would have been much easier. In the process, you would also likely develop highly capable SSKs along the way.
tweets.content:
A Navy veteran once explained the difference between an SSN and an SSBN to a group of fighter-jet enthusiasts:

SSN = Fighter jet
SSBN = Bomber

An SSN is fast, agile, relatively smaller, and most importantly extremely quiet.

An SSBN is a nightmare in hibernation. And once awakened, the target country may end up in hibernation instead.

That’s why the journey from SSBN to SSN is a significant technological leap. Going the other way around would have been much easier. In the process, you would also likely develop highly capable SSKs along the way.

Somnath Mukherjee (@somnath1978)

How much of a "stretch" is an SSN frm an SSBN - instead of mucking arnd w/212 et al, why not crank out 6 SSN?

tweets.title: RT by @OsintTV: राजस्थान ATS की बड़ी कार्रवाई: बबीता से बनी खदीजा जयपुर से गिरफ्तार, पाकिस्तान स्थित आतंकी संगठन जैश-ए-मोहम्मद से संपर्क, मसूद अजहर के साले उस्ताद गौरी से कथित कनेक्शन, ऑनलाइन धर्म परिवर्तन और पाकिस्तान भेजने की साजिश की जांच Rajasthan ATS has arrested Babita Dhakad alias Khadija from Jaipur on charges of maintaining links with Pakistan-based terror outfit Jaish-e-Mohammed. According to investigators, the accused allegedly underwent online religious conversion, adopted a new identity and remained in contact with Pakistani numbers and extremist social media groups. Sources claim she was being groomed by handlers and was allegedly in touch with Ustad Ghouri, the brother-in-law of JeM chief Masood Azhar. Security agencies are now trying to recover deleted digital evidence and investigate whether she was operating as a sleeper cell and if a larger network is active. The accused has been booked under UAPA and remanded to ATS custody till June 27 as multiple agencies continue their probe into possible terror links and foreign connections. #RajasthanATS #JaishEMohammed #Pakistan #Jaipur #NationalSecurity #CounterTerrorism #UAPA #TerrorNetwork #IndianSecurity #BreakingNews #MasoodAzhar #SleeperCell #ATS #IndiaNews #TerrorLinks #Jaish #SecurityAlert #PakistanConnection #AntiTerrorOperation #TINNetwork
tweets.content:
राजस्थान ATS की बड़ी कार्रवाई: बबीता से बनी खदीजा जयपुर से गिरफ्तार, पाकिस्तान स्थित आतंकी संगठन जैश-ए-मोहम्मद से संपर्क, मसूद अजहर के साले उस्ताद गौरी से कथित कनेक्शन, ऑनलाइन धर्म परिवर्तन और पाकिस्तान भेजने की साजिश की जांच
Rajasthan ATS has arrested Babita Dhakad alias Khadija from Jaipur on charges of maintaining links with Pakistan-based terror outfit Jaish-e-Mohammed. According to investigators, the accused allegedly underwent online religious conversion, adopted a new identity and remained in contact with Pakistani numbers and extremist social media groups. Sources claim she was being groomed by handlers and was allegedly in touch with Ustad Ghouri, the brother-in-law of JeM chief Masood Azhar. Security agencies are now trying to recover deleted digital evidence and investigate whether she was operating as a sleeper cell and if a larger network is active. The accused has been booked under UAPA and remanded to ATS custody till June 27 as multiple agencies continue their probe into possible terror links and foreign connections.

#RajasthanATS #JaishEMohammed #Pakistan #Jaipur #NationalSecurity #CounterTerrorism #UAPA #TerrorNetwork #IndianSecurity #BreakingNews #MasoodAzhar #SleeperCell #ATS #IndiaNews #TerrorLinks #Jaish #SecurityAlert #PakistanConnection #AntiTerrorOperation #TINNetwork

tweets.title: BREAKING: Pakistan Threatens India With Military Action Over Indus Waters Pakistan's Defence Minister Khawaja Asif has claimed that Islamabad could resort to war if its water security is threatened, linking the issue directly to national security amid the ongoing dispute over the Indus Waters Treaty. “The moment we feel that our national security, and water is part of our national security, is being threatened, we will go to war against India. Definitely,” he said. The remarks come more than a year after India suspended the treaty following the Pahalgam terror attack, a move that has intensified pressure on Pakistan's already worsening water situation.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: Pakistan Threatens India With Military Action Over Indus Waters

Pakistan's Defence Minister Khawaja Asif has claimed that Islamabad could resort to war if its water security is threatened, linking the issue directly to national security amid the ongoing dispute over the Indus Waters Treaty.

“The moment we feel that our national security, and water is part of our national security, is being threatened, we will go to war against India. Definitely,” he said.

The remarks come more than a year after India suspended the treaty following the Pahalgam terror attack, a move that has intensified pressure on Pakistan's already worsening water situation.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: A country, company, or organization that has the capability to successfully reverse engineer a complex system and manufacture it at scale is often much closer to developing its own indigenous version than many people realize. Reverse engineering is not magic; it requires deep expertise in materials, design, testing, manufacturing, and quality control. Once you have reached that level, creating an original product becomes a matter of time, investment, and intent. The real challenge is building the capability and the determination to do it. Those who lack that capability often come up with reasons why it cannot be done.
tweets.content:
A country, company, or organization that has the capability to successfully reverse engineer a complex system and manufacture it at scale is often much closer to developing its own indigenous version than many people realize.

Reverse engineering is not magic; it requires deep expertise in materials, design, testing, manufacturing, and quality control. Once you have reached that level, creating an original product becomes a matter of time, investment, and intent.

The real challenge is building the capability and the determination to do it. Those who lack that capability often come up with reasons why it cannot be done.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: The €100-billion Future Combat Air System (FCAS/SCAF) program has collapsed following the decision by Germany and France to abandon the joint development of the core New Generation Fighter. Irreconcilable industrial disputes between Dassault Aviation and Airbus, combined with diverging military requirements for the aircraft, led to the project's failure.
tweets.content:
The €100-billion Future Combat Air System (FCAS/SCAF) program has collapsed following the decision by Germany and France to abandon the joint development of the core New Generation Fighter.

Irreconcilable industrial disputes between Dassault Aviation and Airbus, combined with diverging military requirements for the aircraft, led to the project's failure.

tweets.title: Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief: 21st Jun 2026: - We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East - ⁠Follow us for daily Brief ● US VP Vance arrived at Bürgenstock, Switzerland, for direct talks with an Iranian delegation led by Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf and FM Abbas Araghchi. ● Following Iran’s announcement closing the Strait, no vessels have traversed the waterway, despite contradicting US claims. ● Three Chinese-linked oil tankers - ELVA, VIRGO, and VIGOR - laden with Iranian oil are heading toward the Strait of Hormuz. ● Pakistan's FM Ishaq Dar stated that an agreement has been reached between the US and Iran to lower the enrichment level of Tehran’s highly enriched uranium stockpile. ● A product tanker was approached by a skiff with five armed individuals attempting to board, 50 nautical miles southeast of Yemen. END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
21st Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● US VP Vance arrived at Bürgenstock, Switzerland, for direct talks with an Iranian delegation led by Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf and FM Abbas Araghchi.

● Following Iran’s announcement closing the Strait, no vessels have traversed the waterway, despite contradicting US claims.

● Three Chinese-linked oil tankers - ELVA, VIRGO, and VIGOR - laden with Iranian oil are heading toward the Strait of Hormuz.

● Pakistan's FM Ishaq Dar stated that an agreement has been reached between the US and Iran to lower the enrichment level of Tehran’s highly enriched uranium stockpile.

● A product tanker was approached by a skiff with five armed individuals attempting to board, 50 nautical miles southeast of Yemen.

END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: If you look at the bigger picture, a successful outcome from the Iran–US talks and the reopening of Hormuz would be a net positive for India. I understand why some are focused on Pakistan’s attempts to market itself as a “peace broker.” The reality, however, is that Pakistan will still have to go knocking on the doors of the IMF, the Gulf states, the US, or China for economic support. If the global environment becomes conducive to business again, it benefits not just India, but also China, Europe, and ASEAN economies over the longer term. & for that if you have to give this guy Nobel give it.
tweets.content:
If you look at the bigger picture, a successful outcome from the Iran–US talks and the reopening of Hormuz would be a net positive for India.

I understand why some are focused on Pakistan’s attempts to market itself as a “peace broker.” The reality, however, is that Pakistan will still have to go knocking on the doors of the IMF, the Gulf states, the US, or China for economic support.

If the global environment becomes conducive to business again, it benefits not just India, but also China, Europe, and ASEAN economies over the longer term.

& for that if you have to give this guy Nobel give it.
